title: Shinylive vs Traditional Shiny
text:
Shinylive is a way to run Shiny apps directly in a web browser without needing a server. Instead of hosting the app on a platform like shinyapps.io, the app runs entirely on the client side using WebAssembly.
This means the app can be embedded directly into a website while still remaining interactive.









Note



Core Difference:
Traditional Shiny requires a server. Shinylive runs entirely in the browser.





Requires a server (e.g., shinyapps.io)

Processes user input on the server

Supports complex computations and larger datasets





No server required

Runs entirely in the browser

Designed for lightweight, shareable apps












Tip



Shinylive simplifies deployment and reduces friction for smaller projects.



No hosting required
Can be deployed as part of a static website
Lower cost
No need to pay for server infrastructure
Easy to share
Can be embedded directly into a webpage
Fast for simple apps
Runs locally in the browser











Warning



Shinylive is not a replacement for full-scale Shiny applications.



Limited scalability
Not ideal for large datasets
Performance constraints
Browser execution is less powerful than a server
Package limitations
Not all R packages are supported
Less flexibility
Advanced features are harder to implement








building lightweight apps

creating portfolios or demos

sharing simple interactive analyses

teaching or presenting concepts





working with large datasets

building production-level applications

needing full R package support

requiring advanced interactivity






Shinylive is a strong option for simple, shareable applications that do not require server infrastructure. However, traditional Shiny remains the better choice for more complex and scalable applications.
Choosing between the two depends on the size, complexity, and purpose of the project.

title: Shiny App: ODI Grips Content Explorer
text:
Important



This Shiny app was created to provide an interactive exploration tool for ODI Grips Instagram content performance. Unlike the static dashboard, this app allows users to control the analysis by adjusting filters and exploring different performance dimensions in real time.




This Shiny app was built to provide an interactive way to explore ODI Grips Instagram performance data. Unlike the static dashboard from Project 1, this app allows users to dynamically filter and analyze the data based on their own interests.
The goal of the app is to help users understand how content strategy decisions affect performance across different metrics such as reach, engagement, and follower growth.










Note



A static dashboard is strong for summarizing a story. A Shiny app is stronger when the audience needs to explore multiple versions of that story on their own.


A static dashboard is useful for showing overall trends, but it limits the user to a fixed view of the data. In contrast, a Shiny app allows users to:

filter by date range

choose different performance metrics

compare media types

compare caption strategies

explore top-performing posts

This makes the analysis more flexible and more valuable for decision-making.




The app uses ODI Grips Instagram post-level data, including:

posting date

media type

caption type

reach and engagement metrics

follower growth

engagement rate

These variables allow users to explore how different content strategies are associated with different outcomes.






Tip



Because the app is interactive, one dataset can support many different user questions without needing separate reports for each one.






The app includes several interactive components:

KPI summary boxes that update based on filters

trend analysis showing how performance changes over time

media type comparison to evaluate which formats perform best

caption type comparison to analyze messaging strategy

top posts table to identify the highest-performing content

Users can adjust filters and immediately see how results change, making the app more useful than a static report.






Users are not locked into one view of the data. They can change filters and metrics based on what they want to investigate.



The app helps users compare content strategies more directly, which makes it more useful for marketing decisions.



Because the charts and table update instantly, relationships between timing, content format, and performance become easier to identify.











Warning



The main takeaway from this app is that Instagram performance is not driven by one single strategy. Performance varies across content format, caption approach, and timing.


These relationships become much easier to understand when the user can interact with the data directly instead of relying only on a fixed summary dashboard.




For ODI Grips, the value of this app is that it supports a more adaptive content strategy. Instead of assuming one content type always performs best, the app allows users to test how performance changes under different conditions and evaluate which combinations are strongest.
This makes the app useful not only as a class project, but also as a decision-support tool for content planning.




You can access the full interactive app here:
ODI Grips Shiny App




Shiny adds value because it turns a static analysis into an interactive experience. For this project, it makes ODI Grips Instagram data more flexible, more informative, and more practical for real decision-making.

title: Jeffrey Hsu
text:
I am currently pursuing a Master of Science in Digital Marketing at Cal Poly Pomona, with a focus on marketing analytics, dashboard reporting, digital strategy, and data storytelling.
My portfolio is designed for employers and professionals looking for someone who can connect marketing strategy with data-driven decision-making. I am interested in roles related to marketing analytics, performance marketing, digital strategy, campaign reporting, and content performance analysis.

title: Jeffrey Hsu
text:
Career Focus
I want to build a career where I can use data to improve marketing decisions. My strongest interest is in helping organizations understand what is working, what is underperforming, and where they should focus next.
I am especially interested in marketing performance dashboards, attribution analysis, website traffic analytics, SEO and paid media reporting, and turning technical analysis into clear business recommendations that teams can actually use.

title: Jeffrey Hsu
text:
Portfolio Highlights
This website includes selected projects from my digital marketing and analytics coursework.






Note



The goal of this portfolio is to demonstrate both creative digital marketing thinking and technical marketing analytics execution.



Digital Marketing
My digital marketing work focuses on campaign strategy, content planning, SEO, audience targeting, and customer engagement. These projects highlight how I approach marketing from both a business and communication perspective.


Marketing Analytics
My analytics work focuses on dashboards, attribution modeling, website traffic analysis, and reproducible reporting using tools such as R, Quarto, GA4, BigQuery, and Looker Studio.


Presentation Work
The presentation section demonstrates my ability to explain technical concepts clearly to non-technical audiences through professional slide design and data storytelling.

title: Jeffrey Hsu
text:
Skills

Marketing & Analytics
KPI Analysis and Performance Tracking
Experience interpreting campaign and marketing performance metrics to support business decisions.
Content and Social Media Analysis
Ability to evaluate content engagement, audience behavior, and digital marketing performance.
Campaign Evaluation and Optimization
Experience identifying trends, strengths, and opportunities within marketing campaigns.


Technical Skills
R Programming
Data cleaning, visualization, reporting, and basic modeling using R and Quarto.
Dashboard Development
Building dashboards and reproducible reports using Quarto, GA4, BigQuery, and Looker Studio.
Data Visualization
Creating clear charts, dashboards, and presentations for technical and non-technical audiences.


Communication
Business Storytelling
Translating complex analysis into understandable business recommendations.
Presentation Skills
Presenting dashboards, reports, and findings in a professional and audience-friendly format.
Strategic Thinking
Connecting marketing performance insights with broader business goals and strategy.

title: Jeffrey Hsu
text:
Education

California State Polytechnic University, Pomona
Master of Science in Digital Marketing
Coursework and projects have focused on digital marketing strategy, marketing analytics, dashboard development, attribution modeling, web analytics, and reproducible reporting.

title: Jeffrey Hsu
text:
Professional Direction
I want to work in a role where I can combine marketing, analytics, and communication to help teams make smarter, data-informed decisions through reporting, dashboards, and strategic analysis.

title: Jeffrey Hsu
text:
Explore My Work
Use the navigation menu to explore my digital marketing projects, marketing analytics projects, resume, RevealJS presentations, and website traffic analytics work.
